10	# Feature: Multi-Node Dashboard UI
11	
12	**ID:** FEAT-021
13	**Status:** In Progress
14	**Priority:** P1
15	**Owner:** DDx Team
16	
17	## Overview
18	
19	Extends the FEAT-008 web UI with a node/project-aware dashboard. The UI can
20	show a combined view of beads and DDx run activity across all registered
21	projects on a node, or narrow to a specific project for context-dependent
22	views (artifact browser, dependency graph, run history, commit log). Node,
23	project, and page are all embedded in the URL so every view is bookmarkable
24	and shareable.
25	
26	The dashboard UI is implemented as a SvelteKit application with graphql-request for
27	GraphQL data fetching. Every page uses `+page.ts` load functions to fetch data
28	from the `/graphql` endpoint defined in SD-022.
29	
30	## Problem Statement
31	
32	**Current situation:** The FEAT-008 UI is implicitly scoped to the single
33	project the server was started in. There is no node concept, no project picker,
34	and no combined cross-project view for beads or layer-aware run history.
35	
36	**Pain points:**
37	- Operator cannot see all active work across projects in one dashboard
38	- Navigating to a different project requires restarting the server in that
39	  project's directory
40	- Deep links to a specific bead, artifact, or run record are not stable because the URL
41	  carries no project context
42	- Queue-drain evidence is flattened as agent sessions instead of preserving
43	  the `work` -> `try` -> `run` hierarchy from FEAT-010
44	- Node identity is invisible in the UI — there is no indication of which
45	  machine or server instance is being viewed
46	
47	**Desired outcome:** A single `ddx server` UI that surfaces everything
48	happening on the node. The operator can scan the full bead queue and run
49	history across all projects, drill into a specific project for artifact-level
50	work, and bookmark any view with a stable URL.
51	
52	## URL Structure
53	
54	All routes embed context so every view is directly addressable:
55	
56	```
57	/                                     → redirect to /nodes/:nodeId
58	/nodes/:nodeId                        → node overview (health, project list)
59	/nodes/:nodeId/runs                   → combined run history (all projects)
60	/nodes/:nodeId/beads                  → combined bead queue (all projects)
61	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId                   → project overview
62	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/beads             → project beads
63	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/artifacts         → artifact browser
64	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/documents         → document browser
65	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/graph             → doc dependency graph
66	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/commits           → commit log
67	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs              → project run history
68	/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs/:runId       → project run detail
69	```
70	
71	**Combined views** (`/nodes/:nodeId/runs`, `/nodes/:nodeId/beads`) operate
72	across all registered projects. They call node-scoped read APIs for beads and
73	the unified run substrate (since one server = one node). A future multi-node
74	coordinator would fan these out across nodes.
75	
76	**Project-scoped views** (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/...`) pass the
77	project ID to the existing `/api/projects/:project/...` API routes defined in
78	SD-019. The UI must always resolve which project context to bind before
79	making API calls.
80	
81	The `:nodeId` segment is the stable node ID from `GET /api/node` (e.g.
82	`node-7029e8d6`). The `:projectId` segment is the stable project ID from
83	`GET /api/projects` (e.g. `proj-96d7ea83`).
84	
85	## Views
86	
87	### Node Overview (`/nodes/:nodeId`)
88	
89	- Node name, ID, and uptime
90	- Health summary cards: library status, bead store, doc graph per project
91	- Project list with last-seen timestamp and health badge
92	- Quick links to combined runs and combined beads
93	- Server version and started_at
94	
95	**GraphQL Query:** `node` query from FEAT-020, `projects` query for project list.
96	
97	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/+page.svelte`
98	
99	### Combined Bead Queue (`/nodes/:nodeId/beads`)
100	
101	All beads from all registered projects merged into one view. The project each
102	bead belongs to is shown as a column/badge. Supports all filter/sort/search
103	capabilities from FEAT-008 US-082, applied across projects.
104	
105	- Project filter chip to narrow to one project (updates URL)
106	- Full-text search across all projects (client-side)
107	- Status columns: open, in_progress, closed
108	- Priority sort within each column
109	- Clicking a bead navigates to its project-scoped detail:
110	  `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/beads#:beadId`
111	
112	**GraphQL Query:** `beads` with `projectID:` argument per SD-019.
113	
114	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/beads/+page.svelte`
115	
116	### Combined Run History (`/nodes/:nodeId/runs`)
117	
118	All DDx run records across all registered projects, newest first. The project
119	each record belongs to is shown inline, and records retain their FEAT-010 layer
120	label: `work`, `try`, or `run`.
121	
122	- Project filter to narrow
123	- Harness filter
124	- Layer filter (`work`, `try`, `run`)
125	- Status filter
126	- Time range filter
127	- Click to expand: DDx metadata, native session references, token usage,
128	  parent/child links, and artifact producer links
129	- Same capabilities as FEAT-008 US-086 and US-086b
130	
131	**GraphQL Query:** `runs` from FEAT-010 with optional `projectID` omitted.
132	
133	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/runs/+page.svelte`
134	
135	### Project Overview (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId`)
136	
137	- Project name, path, git remote
138	- Library configured: yes/no
139	- Bead summary: counts by status
140	- Recent run activity: last 5 run records
141	- Quick links to project-scoped views
142	
143	**GraphQL Query:** `project` by ID, `beads` count aggregation, `runs`
144	filtered by project.
145	
146	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/+page.svelte`
147	
148	### Project Beads (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/beads`)
149	
150	Same as FEAT-008 bead views (list, kanban, ready queue, detail) scoped to one
151	project. This is the same UI already specified in FEAT-008 US-082 through
152	US-086, with the URL carrying the project context.
153	
154	**GraphQL Query:** `beadsByProject` with cursor pagination per SD-019.
155	
156	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/beads/+page.svelte`
157	
158	### Artifact Browser (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/artifacts`)
159	
160	FEAT-008 artifact browser scoped to the selected project.
161	
162	**GraphQL Query:** `artifacts` with project context per SD-019.
163	
164	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/+page.svelte`
165	
166	### Document Browser (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/documents`)
167	
168	Compatibility route for markdown-focused document navigation. It redirects to
169	or aliases the project artifact browser with a `media_type=text/markdown`
170	filter; new navigation should use `/artifacts`.
171	
172	**GraphQL Query:** `artifacts` with project context per SD-019.
173	
174	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/documents/+page.svelte`
175	
176	### Document Dependency Graph (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/graph`)
177	
178	FEAT-008 dependency graph view scoped to the selected project.
179	
180	**GraphQL Query:** `docGraph` per SD-019.
181	
182	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/graph/+page.svelte`
183	
184	### Commit Log (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/commits`)
185	
186	Git commit history for the project, served by a new `GET /api/projects/:project/commits`
187	endpoint. Displays:
188	- Commit SHA (short), author, date, subject line
189	- Expand to show full commit message and changed files
190	- Filter by author, date range
191	- Link from a commit to any bead whose `closing_commit_sha` matches
192	
193	**GraphQL Query:** `commits` with cursor pagination per SD-019.
194	
195	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/commits/+page.svelte`
196	
197	### Project Run History (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs`)
198	
199	DDx run records filtered to one project. This is the same UI as the combined
200	run history view with the project context bound in the URL.
201	
202	**GraphQL Query:** `runs` with project filter per FEAT-010.
203	
204	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/runs/+page.svelte`
205	
206	### Project Run Detail (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs/:runId`)
207	
208	Layer-aware run detail for a single `work`, `try`, or `run` record. Work
209	records show child try attempts, try records show child layer-1 runs, and
210	layer-1 runs show prompt/config summary, selected harness/provider/model,
211	power bounds, cost/token/duration signals, evidence links, and produced
212	artifact links.
213	
214	**GraphQL Query:** `run` by id with project filter per FEAT-010.
215	
216	**File:** `src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/runs/[runId]/+page.svelte`
217	
218	## Navigation
219	
220	The global navigation bar shows:
221	- Node name + ID (links to node overview)
222	- Project picker dropdown (populated from `/api/projects`)
223	  - Selecting a project navigates to `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId`
224	  - "All projects" option navigates to combined views
225	- Active page tab: Beads | Artifacts | Graph | Runs | Commits
226	
227	The project picker changes the `:projectId` segment in-place while preserving
228	the current page tab. So switching project while on the Graph tab navigates to
229	the new project's graph directly. When switching from a run detail to another
230	project, the UI falls back to that project's run history unless the same run id
231	exists in the target project.
232	
233	**GraphQL Query:** `projects` for picker options.
234	
235	**File:** `src/lib/components/ProjectPicker.svelte`
236	
237	## Requirements
238	
239	### Functional
240	
241	1. Root `/` redirects to `/nodes/:nodeId` using the ID from `GET /api/node`.
242	2. All URLs listed in the URL Structure section are valid deep links — loading
243	   them directly in a browser renders the correct view with data.
244	3. The node overview page is populated from `GET /api/node` and
245	   `GET /api/projects`.
246	4. Combined bead view fetches all projects' beads client-side and merges them.
247	5. Combined run view fetches node-wide run records from the unified run
248	   substrate and preserves `work`, `try`, and `run` layer labels.
249	6. Project-scoped views bind all API calls to
250	   `/api/projects/:project/...` per SD-019.
251	7. The project picker is present on every project-scoped page and updates the
252	   URL when changed.
253	8. A new `GET /api/projects/:project/commits` endpoint serves git commit
254	   history for the project.
255	9. Commit log view links commits to matching beads where
256	   `closing_commit_sha` matches.
257	10. All filter/sort/search state is stored in URL query parameters so filtered
258	    views are also bookmarkable.
259	11. Project run detail routes support parent/child drill-down:
260	    `work` -> `try` -> `run`, with breadcrumbs back up the hierarchy.
261	12. Project artifact routes expose FEAT-008 media-type rendering and link to
262	    producing runs when `generated_by` is present.
263	
264	### Non-Functional
265	
266	- Combined bead view with 500 beads across 5 projects renders in <500ms
267	  (client-side merge).
268	- Navigation between views within the same node+project uses client-side
269	  routing (no full page reload).
270	- The UI degrades gracefully when a project becomes unavailable: its beads and
271	  run records are shown with a stale badge rather than being silently dropped.
272	
273	## User Stories
274	
275	### US-090: Operator Views All Active Work in One Dashboard
276	**As an** operator managing multiple projects on one machine
277	**I want** to see all open beads and recent run records from every project
278	**So that** I can understand the full workload without switching context
279	
280	**Acceptance Criteria:**
281	- Given multiple projects are registered, when I open `/nodes/:nodeId/beads`,
282	  then I see beads from all projects with a project badge on each
283	- Given I filter by project, then only beads from that project are shown and
284	  the URL updates to reflect the filter
285	- Given I open `/nodes/:nodeId/runs`, then I see run records from all projects
286	  merged and sorted by time with a project badge and a layer badge (`work`,
287	  `try`, or `run`) on each record
288	- Given I click a run record, then I navigate to that run's project-scoped
289	  detail at `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs/:runId`; the `projectId`
290	  is carried in each run record so the combined list can form this URL without
291	  a separate lookup; I see layer-appropriate detail: queue inputs for `work`,
292	  bead/worktree fields for `try`, execution summary for `run`
293	- Given I navigate back from a run detail, then the combined run list is
294	  restored with the same sort and filter state
295	
296	**E2E Test:** `node-beads.spec.ts` and `node-runs.spec.ts` — full workflows:
297	- node-beads: open combined beads → verify cross-project beads with project badges → filter by project → verify URL and filtered results → click bead → verify navigation to project-scoped bead detail → press Back
298	- node-runs: open combined runs → verify cross-project records with layer and project badges → filter by layer → click work record → verify detail → click child try → verify fields → press Back through hierarchy
299	
300	### US-091: Operator Navigates to a Project-Scoped View
301	**As an** operator investigating one project
302	**I want** to select a project and see its artifacts, graph, runs, and commits
303	**So that** I can do project-specific work without losing the node context
304	
305	**Acceptance Criteria:**
306	- Given I am on the node overview, when I click a project, then I navigate to
307	  that project's overview at `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId`
308	- Given I am on the artifact browser for project A, when I switch projects in
309	  the picker, then I navigate to the artifact browser for project B without
310	  losing the current page context (e.g. still on `/artifacts`)
311	- Given I copy the current URL and open it in a new tab, then I see the same
312	  view with the same project and page selected
313	- Given I switch projects while on a run detail, then the UI falls back to
314	  that project's run history
315	
316	**E2E Test:** `navigation.spec.ts` — full workflow: open node overview → click project → verify project overview URL → open artifact browser → switch project in picker → verify URL and content updated to project B → copy URL → open in new tab → verify same view → navigate to run detail → switch project → verify fallback to run history
317	
318	### US-092: Operator Bookmarks a Filtered View
319	**As an** operator who checks the ready queue every morning
320	**I want** to bookmark the ready queue filtered to my project
321	**So that** I can open it directly without navigating through the UI
322	
323	**Acceptance Criteria:**
324	- Given I filter the bead list to status=open and project=ddx, then the URL
325	  contains those filter parameters
326	- Given I reload the page with that URL, then the same filter is applied
327	  without any interaction
328	- Given I share the URL with a colleague on the same machine, then they see
329	  the same view
330	
331	**E2E Test:** `navigation.spec.ts` — full workflow: apply status + project filters → verify URL params → copy URL → reload page → verify same filter applied without interaction
332	
333	### US-093: Operator Traces a Bead to Its Closing Commit
334	**As an** operator reviewing completed work
335	**I want** to see the git commit that closed a bead
336	**So that** I can inspect the code change that fulfilled the bead's acceptance
337	
338	**Acceptance Criteria:**
339	- Given a bead has `closing_commit_sha` set, when I view the commit log, then
340	  that commit is highlighted and linked to the bead
341	- Given I click the commit link from the bead detail, then I navigate to the
342	  commit log with that commit in view
343	- Given I click the bead link from the commit row, then I navigate to that
344	  bead's detail
345	
346	**E2E Test:** `commits.spec.ts` — full workflow: open bead with closing commit → click commit link → verify commit log opens with commit highlighted → click bead link from commit row → verify bead detail opens → press Back → verify commit log restored at same position
347	
348	### US-094: Operator Sees Node Identity in the UI
349	**As an** operator with multiple machines
350	**I want** to see which node I am connected to
351	**So that** I don't confuse the eitri server with the bragi server
352	
353	**Acceptance Criteria:**
354	- Given I open the web UI, then the node name and ID are visible in the
355	  navigation bar or node overview
356	- Given the URL contains `:nodeId`, then it matches the ID returned by
357	  `GET /api/node`
358	
359	**E2E Test:** `navigation.spec.ts` — full workflow: open UI → verify node name and ID visible in nav → verify URL nodeId matches GET /api/node response
360	
361	### US-094b: Operator Opens Layer-Aware Run Routes
362	**As an** operator reviewing DDx execution evidence
363	**I want** project-scoped run routes for history and detail
364	**So that** queue drains, bead attempts, and single agent invocations remain
365	  distinct in the URL and UI
366	
367	**Acceptance Criteria:**
368	- Given I navigate to `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs`, then I see
369	  project-scoped run records with filter chips for layer, status, bead,
370	  harness, and time range; active filters are encoded in the URL
371	- Given I click a `work` record, then I navigate to
372	  `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs/:runId` and see the work detail:
373	  queue inputs, selected beads, stop condition, and child try attempts
374	- Given I click a child `try`, then I see bead id, base/result revisions,
375	  worktree path, merge/preserve result, checks, and child layer-1 runs
376	- Given I click a layer-1 `run`, then I see prompt/config summary, power
377	  bounds, selected harness/provider/model, tokens, cost, duration, output, and
378	  evidence links
379	- Given I am viewing any run detail, then breadcrumbs show the full
380	  `work → try → run` path and each crumb is a navigable link
381	- Given a run produced an artifact, then the detail route links to the artifact
382	  browser entry and that artifact links back to the producing run
383	
384	**E2E Test:** `runs.spec.ts` — full workflow: navigate to project runs → apply layer filter → verify URL → click work record → verify work detail fields → click child try → verify try fields → click layer-1 run → verify all fields → follow evidence link → press Back through breadcrumbs to run list → verify filter state restored → follow artifact link from run → verify artifact links back to run
385	
386	## New API Required
387	
388	In addition to the endpoints delivered by FEAT-020 and FEAT-010, FEAT-021
389	requires:
390	
391	| Endpoint | Description |
392	|----------|-------------|
393	| `GET /api/projects/:project/commits` | Git log for the project; supports `?limit=&since=&author=` |
394	
395	All other data is served by endpoints already defined in FEAT-002, FEAT-004,
396	FEAT-006, FEAT-008, FEAT-010, and SD-019.
397	
398	## Implementation Notes
399	
400	### Router
401	
402	SvelteKit handles routing automatically based on `src/routes/` file structure.
403	Routes are nested using the folder hierarchy:
404	
405	```
406	src/routes/
407	├── +layout.svelte              → Node+Project layout with nav
408	├── +page.ts                    → Root redirect to /nodes/:nodeId
409	└── nodes/
410	    └── [$nodeId]/
411	        ├── +layout.ts          → Load node context
412	        ├── +page.svelte        → NodeOverview
413	        ├── beads/              → CombinedBeads
414	        │   └── +page.svelte
415	        ├── runs/               → CombinedRuns
416	        │   └── +page.svelte
417	        └── projects/
418	            └── [$projectId]/
419	                ├── +page.svelte      → ProjectOverview
420	                ├── beads/            → ProjectBeads
421	                │   └── +page.svelte
422	                ├── artifacts/        → ArtifactBrowser
423	                │   └── +page.svelte
424	                ├── documents/        → DocumentBrowser
425	                │   └── +page.svelte
426	                ├── graph/            → DocGraph
427	                │   └── +page.svelte
428	                ├── commits/          → CommitLog
429	                │   └── +page.svelte
430	                └── runs/             → ProjectRuns
431	                    ├── +page.svelte
432	                    └── [$runId]/
433	                        └── +page.svelte
434	```
435	
436	The `[$nodeId]` and `[$projectId]` dynamic route segments capture URL parameters.
437	SvelteKit's `+layout.ts` and `+page.ts` files define load functions that
438	fetch data via graphql-request.
439	
440	### Data Layer
441	
442	graphql-request is the GraphQL client for data fetching, with graphql-ws for
443	subscriptions. Pages define queries inline using the `gql` tagged template
444	and manually typed response interfaces in `+page.ts` load functions.
445	
446	Example bead list query:
447	
448	```typescript
449	// src/routes/nodes/$nodeId/beads/+page.ts
450	import { gql } from 'graphql-request';
451	import { createClient } from '$lib/gql/client';
452	
453	const BEAD_LIST_QUERY = gql`
454	  query BeadList($projectID: ID) {
455	    beads(projectID: $projectID, first: 50) {
456	      edges {
457	        node {
458	          id
459	          title
460	          status
461	          priority
462	          projectID
463	        }
464	      }
465	    }
466	  }
467	`;
468	
469	export const load = async ({ fetch, params }) => {
470	  const client = createClient(fetch);
471	  const data = await client.request(BEAD_LIST_QUERY, { projectID: params.projectId });
472	  return { beads: data.beads };
473	};
474	```
475	
476	graphql-ws subscription support enables real-time updates without polling.
477	
478	Example project run query:
479	
480	```typescript
481	// src/routes/nodes/$nodeId/projects/$projectId/runs/+page.ts
482	import { gql } from 'graphql-request';
483	import { createClient } from '$lib/gql/client';
484	
485	const RUN_LIST_QUERY = gql`
486	  query ProjectRuns($projectID: ID!, $layer: RunLayer) {
487	    runs(projectID: $projectID, layer: $layer, first: 50) {
488	      edges {
489	        node {
490	          id
491	          layer
492	          status
493	          beadID
494	          artifactID
495	          parentRunID
496	          startedAt
497	          completedAt
498	        }
499	      }
500	    }
501	  }
502	`;
503	
504	export const load = async ({ fetch, params, url }) => {
505	  const client = createClient(fetch);
506	  const layer = url.searchParams.get('layer');
507	  const data = await client.request(RUN_LIST_QUERY, {
508	    projectID: params.projectId,
509	    layer
510	  });
511	  return { runs: data.runs };
512	};
513	```
514	
515	### Project Context
516	
517	The `ProjectStore` (using Svelte 5 runes) stores the selected node ID and
518	project ID. It's populated from URL params by `+layout.ts` load functions:
519	
520	```typescript
521	// src/lib/stores/project.ts
522	import { writable } from 'svelte/store';
523	
524	export const projectStore = writable<{
525	  nodeId: string;
526	  projectId?: string;
527	} | null>(null);
528	```
529	
530	Components read from `projectStore` to construct API URLs and filter queries.
531	
532	### Testing
533	
534	Playwright e2e tests verify the complete end-to-end workflow for each user
535	story. Each spec covers the full flow: entry point → actions → outcome →
536	navigation back. Tests use real ddx-server with seeded fixture data; no
537	mocking of the GraphQL or HTTP layer.
538	
539	| E2E Spec | User Stories | Workflow Covered |
540	|---|---|---|
541	| `navigation.spec.ts` | US-091, US-092, US-094 | Root redirect → node overview → project picker → project switch preserving page context → bookmarkable URL round-trip → node identity visible |
542	| `node-beads.spec.ts` | US-090 (beads) | Combined bead view → cross-project badges → filter by project → click bead → navigate to project-scoped detail → Back |
543	| `node-runs.spec.ts` | US-090 (runs) | Combined run history → cross-project layer badges → filter → drill work→try→run → breadcrumbs back to list |
544	| `runs.spec.ts` | US-094b | Project runs → apply layer filter → work detail → try detail → run detail → evidence link → artifact link → Back through full hierarchy |
545	| `commits.spec.ts` | US-093 | Bead with closing commit → commit log → highlighted commit → bead cross-link → Back |
546	
547	## Dependencies
548	
549	- FEAT-008 (web UI foundation — build pipeline, embed pattern, existing views)
550	- FEAT-020 (node identity and project registry — required for /api/node and /api/projects)
551	- SD-019 (project-scoped API routing — /api/projects/:project/...)
552	- FEAT-004 (beads)
553	- FEAT-006 (agent service metadata and native session references)

1054	"""RunLayer identifies which layer of the work/try/run hierarchy a Run belongs to"""
1055	enum RunLayer {
1056	  """Top-level queue drain: encompasses one or more try attempts"""
1057	  work
1058	  """Single bead attempt: encompasses one or more run invocations"""
1059	  try
1060	  """Single agent invocation with a specific harness/model"""
1061	  run
1062	}

1064	"""
1065	Run is a record in the three-layer work→try→run substrate from the FEAT-010
1066	agent execute workflow. It is distinct from ExecutionRun (definition-driven
1067	check/benchmark results) and Execution (execute-bead bundle).
1068	"""
1069	type Run implements Node {
1070	  """Stable identifier for this run record"""
1071	  id: ID!

1073	  layer: RunLayer!

1075	  status: String!

1087	  childRunIds: [String!]!
1088

1091	  queueInputs: JSON
1092	  """Stop condition for the queue drain (work layer only)"""
1093	  stopCondition: String
1094	  """Bead IDs selected for this queue drain (work layer only)"""
1095	  selectedBeadIds: [String!]
1096	
1097	  # ── Try-layer fields (populated when layer = try) ──────────────────────

1101	  resultRevision: String

1103	  worktreePath: String

4	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
5	// Subscription client singleton
6	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
7	
8	let _subClient: Client | null = null
9	
10	function resolveWsUrl(): string {
11		if (typeof window === 'undefined') {
12			// Node / SSR — subscriptions are client-only, so this path is never
13			// reached in practice, but we provide a sensible default for testing.
14			return 'ws://localhost:7743/graphql'
15		}
16		const proto = window.location.protocol === 'https:' ? 'wss' : 'ws'
17		return `${proto}://${window.location.host}/graphql`
18	}
19	
20	/**
21	 * Returns the shared graphql-ws client instance, creating it on first call.
22	 * Accepts an optional URL override (used in tests).
23	 */
24	export function getSubscriptionClient(urlOverride?: string): Client {
25		if (!_subClient) {
26			_subClient = createClient({ url: urlOverride ?? resolveWsUrl() })
27			_subClient.on('connecting', () => wsConnection._onConnecting())
28			_subClient.on('connected', () => wsConnection._onConnected())
29			_subClient.on('closed', () => wsConnection._onClosed())
30		}
31		return _subClient
32	}
33	
34	/** Tear down the singleton — call between tests or on hot-reload. */
35	export function disposeSubscriptionClient(): void {
36		if (_subClient) {
37			_subClient.dispose()
38			_subClient = null
39		}
40		wsConnection._reset()
41	}

48		eventID: string

51		kind: string

56		timestamp: string

72	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
73	
74	const BEAD_LIFECYCLE_SUBSCRIPTION = `
75	subscription BeadLifecycle($projectID: ID!) {
76	  beadLifecycle(projectID: $projectID) {
77	    eventID
78	    beadID
79	    kind
80	    summary
81	    timestamp
82	  }
83	}
84	`
85	
86	/**
87	 * Subscribe to live lifecycle events for all beads in a project.
88	 *
89	 * The watcher polls beads.jsonl on disk; events arrive within ~1 s of a change.
90	 * Returns a dispose function — call it to unsubscribe and free resources.
91	 *
92	 * @example
93	 * const dispose = subscribeBeadLifecycle('/path/to/project', (evt) => {
94	 *   if (evt.kind === 'status_changed') updateStatus(evt.beadID, evt.summary)
95	 * })
96	 * onDestroy(dispose)
97	 */
98	export function subscribeBeadLifecycle(
99		projectID: string,
100		onEvent: (event: BeadEvent) => void,
101		onError?: (err: unknown) => void,
102		onComplete?: () => void
103	): () => void {
104		const client = getSubscriptionClient()
105		return client.subscribe(
106			{ query: BEAD_LIFECYCLE_SUBSCRIPTION, variables: { projectID } },
107			{
108				next(data) {
109					const evt = (data.data as Record<string, unknown> | null | undefined)
110						?.beadLifecycle as BeadEvent | undefined
111					if (evt) onEvent(evt)
112				},
113				error(err) {
114					if (onError) {
115						onError(err)
116					} else {
117						console.error('[ddx] beadLifecycle subscription error:', err)
118					}
119				},
120				complete() {
121					onComplete?.()
122				}
123			}
124		)
125	}

import type { PageLoad } from './$types'
import { createClient } from '$lib/gql/client'
import { gql } from 'graphql-request'

const RUN_DETAIL_QUERY = gql`
	query RunDetail($id: ID!) {
		run(id: $id) {
			id
			layer
			status
			projectID
			beadId
			parentRunId
			childRunIds
			startedAt
			completedAt
			durationMs

			queueInputs
			stopCondition
			selectedBeadIds

			baseRevision
			resultRevision
			worktreePath
			mergeOutcome
			checkResults

			harness
			provider
			model
			promptSummary
			powerMin
			powerMax
			tokensIn
			tokensOut
			costUsd
			outputExcerpt
			evidenceLinks
		}
	}
`

export interface RunDetail {
	id: string
	layer: string
	status: string
	projectID: string | null
	beadId: string | null
	parentRunId: string | null
	childRunIds: string[]
	startedAt: string | null
	completedAt: string | null
	durationMs: number | null

	queueInputs: string | null
	stopCondition: string | null
	selectedBeadIds: string[] | null

	baseRevision: string | null
	resultRevision: string | null
	worktreePath: string | null
	mergeOutcome: string | null
	checkResults: string | null

	harness: string | null
	provider: string | null
	model: string | null
	promptSummary: string | null
	powerMin: number | null
	powerMax: number | null
	tokensIn: number | null
	tokensOut: number | null
	costUsd: number | null
	outputExcerpt: string | null
	evidenceLinks: string[] | null
}

export const load: PageLoad = async ({ params, fetch }) => {
	const client = createClient(fetch as unknown as typeof globalThis.fetch)
	const data = await client.request<{ run: RunDetail | null }>(RUN_DETAIL_QUERY, {
		id: params.runId
	})

	return {
		nodeId: params.nodeId,
		projectId: params.projectId,
		runId: params.runId,
		run: data.run
	}
}

feat(ui): runs list pages with URL-backed filters [ddx-a1d0b136]

Combined /nodes/:nodeId/runs shows cross-project run history with project
and layer badges; project-scoped /nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs

Run detail route /runs/:runId renders layer-appropriate fields (work queue
inputs, try attempt details, run-layer execution summary) with breadcrumb
nav back to the list.

NavShell gains a Runs tab for project-scoped navigation and an All Runs
link for the combined node-wide view.

Backend: adds projectID field to the Run GraphQL type (schema + gqlgen
regeneration) and populates it in loadRunsForProject so combined views can
form correct project-scoped detail URLs without a separate lookup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

Commit succeeded. Let me walk through the acceptance criteria to verify coverage:

**AC1** ✅ `/nodes/:nodeId/runs` renders cross-project run list with project and layer badges — implemented in `nodes/[nodeId]/runs/+page.svelte` with project badge (inline-flex border) and layer badge (colored rounded-full).

**AC2** ✅ `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/runs` renders project-scoped list with filter chips — implemented in `projects/[projectId]/runs/+page.svelte` with layer, status, and harness filter chips.

**AC3** ✅ Filter chip selection updates URL (`goto` with `?layer=...&status=...`) and filters list; reload restores same view via `+page.ts` load function reading `url.searchParams`.

**AC4** ✅ Combined view: row click navigates to correct project-scoped detail URL via `runDetailHref()` which uses `run.projectID` (populated by `tagProjectID()` in `state_runs.go`).

**AC5** ✅ Empty state: `{#if edges.length === 0}` renders "No runs found." in both pages.

**AC6** ✅ NavShell Runs tab highlights on project-scoped `/runs*` routes; "All Runs" sidebar link highlights on `/nodes/:nodeId/runs*` route.
